clientApi.title: Client API access and test bench
clientApi.subtitle: Use this screen to verify the current session scope, inspect the available client endpoints, and run live calls for document retrieval, signed downloads, acknowledgements, and VAUT-only uploads.
clientApi.endpointSearch: Searches files available to the authenticated client within its issuer/entity scope.
clientApi.endpointDownloadLink: Issues a short-lived signed download link for a document already visible to the session.
clientApi.endpointAckHistory: Lists acknowledgements already emitted for the selected document.
clientApi.endpointAckPost: Posts a new acknowledgement update for a document inside the caller scope.
clientApi.endpointUpload: Deposits a source file into the storage tree. Reserved to the VAUT account.
clientApi.accessScopedRead: Access: authenticated client, issuer/entity checks enforced.
clientApi.accessScopedWrite: Access: authenticated client, scoped write allowed on its own documents only.
clientApi.accessVautOnly: Access: ROLE_ADMIN + explicit VAUT account check.
clientApi.searchPlaceholder: File name, ID, or full path
clientApi.noResults: No document returned for this query.
clientApi.downloadTitle: Generate and consume a signed link
clientApi.uploadLocked: This endpoint is reserved to the VAUT account. The current session can inspect it here but cannot execute it.
clientApi.queryRequired: Enter a file name, ID, or path before searching.
clientApi.ackFieldsRequired: Entity code and document ID are required to send an acknowledgement.
clientApi.invalidJson: Invalid JSON. Check the payload syntax.
clientApi.etlSubtitle: Step-by-step guide for programmatic document consumption. Each step is idempotent and restartable — designed for automation tools.
clientApi.adminSubtitle: Full platform access: deposit source files, search the catalog, and manage acknowledgements on behalf of any issuer.
clientApi.etlFlowDesc: Poll the deliveries delta → download each file → acknowledge receipt. The protocol is designed to be restartable at any step without creating duplicates.
clientApi.etlFlowStep1: GET /deliveries?since=... → get new files with sha256 + size
clientApi.etlFlowStep2: GET /documents/{id}/content → download with Bearer token, verify sha256
clientApi.step1Desc: Fetch all documents updated since a given timestamp. The response includes sha256, size, and a direct download URL for each file. Use `nextCursor` from the response to paginate until `hasMore` is false.
clientApi.step1Tip: Store the highest `updatedAt` from results and use it as `since` on the next poll. If `hasMore: true`, paginate completely before processing.
clientApi.step2Desc: Download the file directly using your Bearer token. No signed link needed for machine-to-machine flows. The response includes an ETag header containing the SHA-256 of the file.
clientApi.step2Tip: Compare the ETag value with the `sha256` field from step 1 to detect any corruption in transit.
clientApi.step3Desc: Send an idempotent acknowledgement using PUT. Include an `Idempotency-Key` unique per operation: a retry with the same key returns the original 200 response without any side effect.
clientApi.step3Tip: State machine — valid transitions: AR0 → AR2, AR3, AR4 · AR2 → AR3, AR4 · AR3 and AR4 are terminal (no further transition allowed).
clientApi.batchDesc: Acknowledge up to 200 documents in a single request. Returns 207 Multi-Status with one result per item. Each item is processed independently — a failure does not cancel the others. Each item can carry its own `idempotencyKey`.
clientApi.idempotencyKeyHint: Generate a unique key per operation (UUID recommended). Leave empty to skip idempotency guarantee.
clientApi.hasMore: More results available — use nextCursor to continue pagination.
clientApi.endpointDeliveries: Returns documents updated since a given timestamp, sorted by (updatedAt, fileId). Ideal for incremental polling.
clientApi.endpointDeliveriesExport: Exports all deliveries since a timestamp as JSONL or CSV manifest. Limited to 50 000 documents per call.
clientApi.endpointDirectDownload: Downloads file content directly with Bearer token. HEAD supported for existence/ETag check without data transfer.
clientApi.endpointAckPut: Records an idempotent acknowledgement. Same Idempotency-Key on retry returns the original response (200) without side effects.
clientApi.endpointAckBatch: Records up to 200 acknowledgements in one call. Returns 207 Multi-Status — each item has its own result (OK / ALREADY_APPLIED / ERROR).
clientApi.accessEtlWrite: API access · authenticated, idempotent write on own documents
auth.title: Authenticate to access the single window portal
auth.subtitle: Open a diffusion session to review documents, alerts, and audit trails within your assigned scope.
auth.demoHint: Click a profile to prefill the credentials. In demo mode, the password matches the username.
dash.title: Compliance Overview
dash.subtitle: Real-time regulatory status for Fiscal Year 2024
dash.efficiencyDesc: Your diffusion unit is performing at {rate}% AR-3 completion rate, ensuring regulatory compliance.
alerts.title: Operational Alerts
alerts.subtitle: Daily detection of missing receptions, reconciliation gaps, and missing acknowledgements.
docList.title: Document Catalog
docList.subtitle: Advanced diffusion interface for regulatory compliance documents and audit-ready ledger entries.
audit.title: Audit Trail Ledger
audit.subtitle: Immutable record of system access, document interactions, and administrative decisions.
audit.integrityDesc: No integrity violations detected. All hash chains are valid.
settings.title: System Settings
settings.subtitle: File hierarchy, synchronization, and application settings.
hierarchy.title: Storage Tree
rename.title: Rename File
move.title: Move File
bulk.title: Bulk Rename (extension)
bulk.description: Change the extension of all files in a given directory. Useful for bulk status updates.
info.title: Expected structure
info.description: The file extension determines the AR status (xml, AR2, AR3, AR4). Any modification automatically triggers an H2 index resynchronization.
sync.title: Filesystem → H2 Synchronization
sync.indexDescription: The H2 index is rebuilt from the filesystem. Automatic sync runs every 60 seconds. Use the button below to force an immediate resynchronization.
sync.step1Desc: Recursive traversal of the storage_root/ tree
sync.step2Desc: Period, type, and status extracted from file name and extension
sync.step3Desc: Documents are created/updated in the in-memory database with SHA256 computation
sync.step4Desc: Dashboard statistics are recalculated after each sync
sync.warningDesc: The index is stored in memory (jdbc:h2:mem). It is automatically rebuilt on service startup from the filesystem, which remains the source of truth.
params.entityCodeHint: Used as default filter for documents
params.persistenceDesc: Changes are applied immediately but will be lost on server restart. To make changes permanent, edit the application.properties file.
error.resource_not_found: The requested resource could not be found.
error.ack_already_applied: Acknowledgement already applied for this document.
error.access_denied: You do not have permission to access this resource.
error.internal_server: An unexpected internal server error occurred.
